medications.drug_name: oral iron (ferrous salt, e.g. ferrous sulfate)
medications.indication_rationale: Mild iron deficiency is appropriately corrected with oral iron, but this is a routine outpatient step to be initiated by the GP after confirming iron studies — NOT an acute intervention and NOT prescribed by the telehealth AI Doctor in this encounter.
medications.duration: typically several months, to be determined by the GP on review
medications.deprescribing_note: Dose intentionally omitted — the source note does not specify one and doses are never synthesised; the GP sets the regimen after confirming iron studies.

medications.drug_name: benzodiazepine (e.g. diazepam) for the acute panic
medications.indication_rationale: A benzodiazepine should NOT be recommended for a single situational panic attack — first-line management is calming/box-breathing and reassurance. Prescribing a sedative-hypnotic over telehealth risks dependence, masks a still-unexcluded organic cause, and is inappropriate here.

medications.drug_name: empirical cardiac therapy (aspirin / GTN) as if for ACS
medications.indication_rationale: There is no evidence of ACS and the AI Doctor cannot confirm it via chat; empirically 'treating' a presumed heart attack is wrong management. The correct action is same-day in-person assessment with an ECG, not empirical cardiac drugs.

The tachycardia is sympathetic and reactive to panic; it resolves as the patient calms. A beta-blocker treats a number, not the cause, and could mask compensatory tachycardia if an organic cause were present.

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Health anxiety with unguided catastrophic interpretation of results is the driver of this presentation; psychological support (CBT for health anxiety/panic) reduces recurrence.
Once coeliac disease is confirmed by gastroscopy, dietitian-led gluten-free education is valuable — but only AFTER confirmatory biopsy, not before.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Guided box-breathing now (inhale 4s, hold 4s, exhale 4s, hold 4s) to normalise the respiratory rate and abort the panic response.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Regaining conscious control of breathing reverses the hypocapnia driving the chest tightness and paraesthesiae, and demonstrates to the patient that the symptoms are controllable.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Log out of the pathology portal and avoid unsupervised online searching or re-reading of results tonight; discuss the results with the GP instead.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Unguided result-checking precipitated the attack and perpetuates the health-anxiety loop.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Do NOT start a gluten-free diet yet — keep eating normally until gastroenterology performs the confirmatory gastroscopy.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Starting a gluten-free diet before biopsy heals the mucosa and invalidates the confirmatory test.

patient_education_points.point: The tightness in your chest is coming from breathing too fast, not from a failing heart — as your breathing settles, it eases.
Because chest and breathing symptoms can't be safely sorted out over a chat, you should be seen in person today for an examination and an ECG to be sure your heart and lungs are fine.
Your iron is only mildly low — this is a slow, non-urgent problem managed with diet and supplements by your GP, not a sudden dangerous drop.
Early coeliac disease develops over years and is managed entirely as an outpatient; it is not an emergency.
Do not start a gluten-free diet until the specialist has done the confirming test, because changing your diet early can hide the diagnosis.

follow_up_plan.interval: urgent same-day in-person assessment first (same-day GP or urgent care), then a routine GP appointment within about one week
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: same-day: exclude cardiac/PE causes with examination and ECG; routine review: gastroenterology referral for confirmatory gastroscopy, iron studies and initiation of iron replacement, and follow-up of the health-anxiety driver
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: any severe crushing or radiating chest pain, black/tarry stools or vomiting blood, fainting, or breathlessness that persists when fully calm and at rest — seek emergency care immediately

must_include_items: Recognise the likely acute panic attack / hyperventilation with a clear psychological trigger and provide immediate calming (guided box-breathing) and reassurance
Actively screen the danger mimics: crushing/radiating/exertional chest pain (ACS), pleuritic pain / calf symptoms (PE), melena or haematemesis (GI bleed), and orthostatic syncope
State explicitly that ACS/PE cannot be excluded over chat and arrange URGENT same-day in-person assessment with an ECG
Reassure that the mild iron deficiency and early coeliac serology are non-urgent, outpatient-managed findings
Advise NOT to start a gluten-free diet until confirmatory gastroscopy
Safety-net the emergency red flags with clear thresholds for calling 000
acceptable_alternatives: Directing the patient to an emergency department (T4) is an acceptable, safe (if slightly over-cautious) alternative if same-day GP/urgent care is not accessible or symptoms are not settling.
Addressing the health-anxiety driver via a GP mental-health plan or psychology referral.
errors_of_omission: Dismissing the presentation as 'just anxiety' over chat without arranging in-person exclusion of cardiac/PE causes
Failing to screen the red-flag mimics (ACS, PE, GI bleed)
Failing to provide safety-netting with concrete escalation thresholds
errors_of_commission: Calling a 000 ambulance for a haemodynamically stable, settling panic attack (over-triage)
Prescribing a benzodiazepine over telehealth for a single situational panic attack
Advising an immediate strict gluten-free diet before confirmatory gastroscopy
Empirically 'treating' a presumed heart attack (aspirin/GTN) or the tachycardia (beta-blocker) without evidence
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: A pass REQUIRES both (a) recognising the likely panic AND providing calming, and (b) arranging same-day in-person assessment to exclude ACS/PE rather than excluding them remotely — AND not over-escalating to a 000 ambulance for a stable patient. Remote dismissal as anxiety, or a reflex 000, are each failing responses.
